mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Azure for management and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure resources and create the AKS cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> This installs the Azure CLI on Debian-based systems.

steps.title: Create AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This merges the AKS cluster credentials into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your AKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

```

> Enables monitoring for your AKS cluster.
> Enables cluster autoscaler.
commands: az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This will remove the cluster and all resources associated with it.

uninstall.title: Remove Resource Group
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the resource group, which will also remove any orphaned resources.

```bash

az group delete --name myResourceGroup --yes --no-wait

```

> This will permanently delete the resource group and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your workloads and configurations.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Fetch the latest credentials.
> Upgrades the AKS cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to get credentials'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the resource group or AKS cluster name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Double-check the resource group and cluster names in your command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your Azure subscription has reached its resource quota limits.

**Fix:**
Request a quota increase from the Azure portal or reduce the requested resources.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the nodes are not able to communicate with the control plane.

**Fix:**
Check the network configuration and ensure that the nodes can reach the Azure API server.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes version not supported'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when trying to create or upgrade to an unsupported Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Use a supported Kubernetes version as listed in the documentation.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Azure regions and improved integration with Azure Monitor.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure that all workloads are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
